goal: Land the two G5 (Context) external-binary plugin manifests for Phase 3 Week 10 — `plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml` (P3-W10-F02) and `plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml` (P3-W10-F03) — plus their devtools install hints, an end-to-end `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test exercising real `PluginManager::health_check` round-trips, and replacements for the TODO-stub acceptance scripts at `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh` and `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F03.sh` that pair an MCP `tools/list` smoke with a feature-specific tool-level smoke (F02: resolve-library-id / get-library-docs result-shape; F03: pack_codebase wall-time + ≥60% token-reduction-vs-naive-cat result-shape against `tests/fixtures/rust-project`). The pattern is the WO-0069 (codebase-memory+mem0) paired-manifest template combined with the WO-0072 (codegraphcontext) M1/M2 mutation contract, applied to G5 — so no daemon source-code changes are required. Establishes the new `context.*` capability namespace under `plugins/context/`.
plan_summary: Mirror the WO-0069 paired-manifest precedent verbatim, retargeted from G3 (Knowledge) to G5 (Context). Two stdio MCP servers — Context7 (`npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp`) and Repomix (`npx -y repomix --mcp`) — get hand-authored TOML manifests under a new `plugins/context/<name>/plugin.toml` directory, each pinned to a verified-by-executor npm tag with `transport.type = "stdio"` and a documented `capabilities.provides` list under the `context.*` namespace. Two devtools install scripts (`scripts/devtools/install-context7-mcp.sh`, `scripts/devtools/install-repomix-mcp.sh`) provide hint-only `npm i -g` / `npx --yes` warm-up commands. A new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test file declares one `mod g5_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with two `#[tokio::test] async fn` health-check tests — substring selectors `g5_plugin_manifests::context7_manifest_health_check` and `g5_plugin_manifests::repomix_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly per DEC-0007 + WO-0069 lessons §"For planner: module-root placement". Two `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh` + `P3-W10-F03.sh` scripts replace the TODO stubs and run the targeted `cargo test` selector plus a tool-level smoke (gated by `UCIL_SKIP_CONTEXT_PLUGIN_E2E` per the WO-0072 `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E` precedent, but defaulting to RUN since both upstream packages are public-npm and no API key is required for `tools/list` or the smoke tools). The M1/M2 mutation contract is pre-baked verbatim from WO-0072 §5/§6: M1 poisons `transport.command` for each manifest in turn, M2 replaces the expected canonical tool name (likely `resolve-library-id` for Context7 and `pack_codebase` for Repomix — executor must confirm by reading the live `tools/list` response from each upstream binary BEFORE choosing the literal). No daemon source changes; no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` edits permitted (additive plugins/tests/scripts only).
scope_in: 1. New plugin manifest at `plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml` for the upstream Upstash Context7 MCP server. Required keys: `[plugin] name="context7" category="context" priority="P0" version="<semver>"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="npx" args=["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp@<pinned-tag>"]`; `[capabilities] provides=["context.docs.resolve_library_id", "context.docs.get_library_docs"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names by spawning the live binary and capturing the canonical `tools/list` response BEFORE writing the literal — prefer kebab-case-as-emitted-by-upstream over snake-case translation); `[health]` keys mirror the codegraphcontext pattern. Top-of-file rustdoc comment must document: source URL, npm package name, pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list in the comment per WO-0069 precedent), transport type, API key requirements (Context7 requires NONE for `tools/list`; the doc-fetch tools may make a network call to context7.com but no key needed for free-tier reads — document this verbatim for the verifier).

2. New plugin manifest at `plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml` for the upstream Repomix MCP server. Required keys: `[plugin] name="repomix" category="context" priority="P0" version="<semver>"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="npx" args=["-y", "repomix@<pinned-tag>", "--mcp"]`; `[capabilities] provides=["context.repo_pack.pack_codebase", "context.repo_pack.pack_remote_repository", "context.repo_pack.read_repomix_output"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list`); `[health]` keys mirror the codegraphcontext pattern. Top-of-file rustdoc comment must document: source URL (https://github.com/yamadashy/repomix), npm package name, pinned tag, binary launch command, advertised tools (full live list), transport type, API key requirements (NONE — Repomix is local-only; no network calls for `pack_codebase` against a local path).

3. New devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-context7-mcp.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies node + npm available; runs `npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@<pinned-tag> --help 2>&1 | head -1` (or analogous warm-up that triggers npm fetch + cache without blocking on stdin) to ensure the package is in the npm cache for the integration test's first spawn. Must `set -euo pipefail`; must exit 0 on success; must NOT require sudo. Mirror `scripts/devtools/install-codegraphcontext-mcp.sh` shape verbatim for the structure (top-of-file shebang + license-style comment + dependency check + warm-up + final echo).

4. New devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-repomix-mcp.sh`. Same shape as install-context7-mcp.sh but warming `repomix@<pinned-tag>`. Repomix's `--help` flag is supported on the canonical CLI; use that as the warm-up trigger (NOT `--mcp` since that would spawn the MCP server and block on stdin).

5. New integration-test file at `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs`. Top of file: `#![cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]` if other plugin tests carry it (verify via `head -5 crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs` first); otherwise omit. Single `mod g5_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at file root containing two `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests — `context7_manifest_health_check` and `repomix_manifest_health_check`. Each test: (a) loads the corresponding `plugins/context/<name>/plugin.toml` via the daemon's existing manifest loader (use the same APIs as `g3_plugin_manifests.rs` and `g4_plugin_manifests.rs`); (b) constructs a `PluginManager` (or analogous abstraction) and calls `health_check` (or the equivalent method that round-trips a real MCP `tools/list` over stdio); (c) asserts the live response carries a specific canonical tool name (e.g. `resolve-library-id` for Context7, `pack_codebase` for Repomix — executor confirms BEFORE choosing the literal); (d) panics with `(SAn) <semantic_name>; got: <full-live-tool-list>` on assertion failure per DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format. Substring selectors `g5_plugin_manifests::context7_manifest_health_check` and `g5_plugin_manifests::repomix_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly per `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests <selector>` invocation form. NO `mod tests { ... }` nesting; the test functions live at `mod g5_plugin_manifests` ROOT (NOT inside a nested `mod tests` per DEC-0007 + WO-0069 lessons §"For planner"). Mirror the existing `g4_plugin_manifests.rs` file shape verbatim for the imports, helper-function placement, and tokio runtime flavor declaration.

6. Replace `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh` (currently a TODO stub). New script: `set -euo pipefail`; (a) sanity-check that `plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts the keys named in scope_in #1; (b) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests g5_plugin_manifests::context7_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'`; (c) tool-level smoke: spawn the Context7 MCP server via the canonical `npx` command, send an MCP `initialize` + `tools/list` + `tools/call` JSON-RPC sequence over stdio (the `tools/call` invokes `resolve-library-id` with a fixture-stable library name like `react` or `express` — pick one that resolves stably from the typescript-project fixture's `package.json` direct deps; document the chosen library + WHY in the script's top-of-file comment); assert the response is a successful JSON-RPC reply (id matches, no `error` key) and contains a non-empty `content` field consistent with the F02 spec line `query for a library used in the fixture typescript-project returns current API docs`. Gate the tool-level smoke behind `UCIL_SKIP_CONTEXT_PLUGIN_E2E` env-var per the WO-0072 precedent — but DEFAULT to RUN (NOT skip) since the tool needs no API key and the smoke is the load-bearing F02 acceptance signal. Final exit 0 on success; non-zero on any check failure with a clear `>&2` diagnostic.

7. Replace `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F03.sh` (currently a TODO stub). New script: `set -euo pipefail`; (a) sanity-check that `plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts the keys named in scope_in #2; (b) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests g5_plugin_manifests::repomix_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'`; (c) tool-level smoke specific to F03 acceptance: copy `tests/fixtures/rust-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir; spawn the Repomix MCP server via the canonical `npx` command; send an MCP `initialize` + `tools/list` + `tools/call` JSON-RPC sequence where `tools/call` invokes `pack_codebase` (or whichever canonical tool the live `tools/list` advertises for repo-to-context packing — executor confirms) with the tmpdir path as the target; measure wall-time via `date +%s%N` before/after the `tools/call`; assert wall-time < 5000 ms (per F03 spec line); compute a naive token approximation by `cat $(find $tmpdir -type f) | wc -c` (char-count is a safe proxy for token-count for the ratio comparison; note this approximation in the script comment); compute the packed-output char-count from the `tools/call` response's content payload; assert `(naive - packed) / naive >= 0.60` (≥60% token reduction per F03 spec line). Gate the tool-level smoke behind `UCIL_SKIP_CONTEXT_PLUGIN_E2E` env-var per the WO-0072 precedent — DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0 on success; non-zero with a clear `>&2` diagnostic on any failure.

8. M1 mutation (transport.command poison; runs once per feature). Pre-mutation per feature: `md5sum plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0074-context7-orig.md5` (and analogous for repomix). Mutation: edit the targeted `plugin.toml` and replace `command = "npx"` with `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"`. Selector: the targeted health-check test under `g5_plugin_manifests`. Expected panic: a `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` chain (or analogous `tokio::process::Command::spawn` ENOENT-class error). Restore: `git checkout -- plugins/context/<name>/plugin.toml`. Confirm: `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0074-<name>-orig.md5` returns OK. Mirror the WO-0072 §5 verbatim shape for both manifests in turn.

9. M2 mutation (expected-tool-name regression; runs once per feature). Pre-mutation per feature: `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0074-g5tests-orig.md5`. Mutation: edit the test file and replace whichever string the targeted test asserts as the canonical tool name (e.g. `"resolve-library-id"` for Context7 — confirm by reading the test BEFORE picking the literal, since the executor will have selected it from the live `tools/list` response) with `"NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`. Selector: same. Expected: structured panic carrying the full live tool-list (`(SAn) <semantic>; got: ["<live tool 1>", "<live tool 2>", ...]; want: "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs`. Confirm via md5. Mirror the WO-0072 §6 verbatim shape.

10. Commit cadence: ~50 LOC per commit per the project commit-style.md hard target; aim for 7-10 atomic commits. Suggested order: (i) plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml; (ii) scripts/devtools/install-context7-mcp.sh; (iii) plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml; (iv) scripts/devtools/install-repomix-mcp.sh; (v) crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs (test 1: context7_manifest_health_check); (vi) extend g5_plugin_manifests.rs (test 2: repomix_manifest_health_check); (vii) scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh; (viii) scripts/verify/P3-W10-F03.sh. Each commit Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + `Feature: P3-W10-F02` or `P3-W10-F03` + `Work-order: WO-0074` trailers. Commits exceeding the soft 50-LOC target are acceptable when the file is one cohesive unit (e.g. the rustdoc-rich plugin.toml at ~60-80 LOC) under DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out — cite the carve-out in the commit body if invoked.

11. Standing AC23 substantive coverage protocol per WO-0067..WO-0073 carryover. Use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --no-default-features --features default --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` as the authoritative measurement. WO is purely additive on tests + manifests + scripts — pre-WO baseline must be preserved (no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes per scope_out #2). Document the measurement in the RFR with both the gate-script value and the workaround value if they differ.

12. Apply WO-0073 lessons §"For planner" verbatim: tests at module root (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`); DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format on every assert; DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam pattern for any future production-wiring follow-up (NOT in this WO scope — this WO is manifests-only); cite WO-0069 + WO-0072 + WO-0073 lessons by ID in the RFR's §"Lessons applied" section.
scope_out: 1. NO Aider repo-map (P3-W10-F01) — that's a separate solo WO requiring substantial Rust reimplementation of PageRank over the call graph (master plan §4.5 line 345 `Library (internal, Rust) | P0`). This WO is plugin manifests only.

2. NO `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. WO is purely additive on `plugins/context/`, `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/`, `scripts/devtools/`, `scripts/verify/`. Critic precedent at WO-0071 (DEC-0019) flags forbidden_paths violations on daemon-src changes for plugin-manifest-scoped WOs.

3. NO G5 group runtime in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g5.rs`. That's the WO-0073-equivalent G5 follow-up (G3→G4→G5 template port per WO-0073 lessons §"For planner: G-group recipe") and lands as a separate WO consuming both plugin manifests from this WO. F04 (G5 parallel query) is the dep-blocked feature ID for that follow-up.
4. NO classify_query / parse_reason daemon-side wiring. That follow-up is dep-unblocked per WO-0070 lessons §"For planner" but is orthogonal to this WO's plugin-manifest scope.

5. NO ADR creation under `ucil-build/decisions/**`. ADRs are planner-territory (DEC-0019 critic precedent at WO-0071 attempt-2). The DEC-0019 §1 disposition for graphiti is not relevant to this WO; cite for context only if needed.
6. NO modifications to `tests/fixtures/**`. The fixture rust-project + typescript-project are existing read-only assets — the F03 verify script copies into a tmpdir before mutating per scope_in #7.
7. NO new entries in `feature-list.json`. The F02 + F03 entries already exist with frozen `id` + `description` + `acceptance_tests` + `dependencies` per the no-mid-phase-additions rule.

8. NO P3-W9-F11 (incremental computation integration test) work. Per WO-0073 lessons §"For planner: F11 is orthogonal to G4" — F11 is Salsa-engine + tree-sitter pipeline surface, NOT a context group manifest. Separate WO.
9. NO follow-up daemon spurious-frame-drain code change (DEC-0019 §3 Phase 7 hardening). Unrelated to G5 plugins.
10. NO graphiti P3-W9-F10 plugin manifest. Deferred to Phase 7 per DEC-0019 — not part of this WO.

11. Effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`) — pre-existing standing scope_out, awaiting dedicated harness-improvement WO. WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073 carryover, now 7 WOs deep.

12. `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug (carries from WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073). Workaround unchanged: M1/M2 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer; reality-check.sh is informational only.

13. `scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh` harness improvement (now 30 WOs deep; intermittent — sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER interaction). Continue substantive AC23 coverage check via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround. Bucket B / Bucket D triage candidate.

14. NO production-wiring of `Context7G5Source` / `RepomixG5Source` / `AiderRepoMapG5Source` traits or impls. Those are part of the G5 production-wiring follow-up WO (analogous to G1/G3/G4 production-wiring deferrals per WO-0070/0073 lessons).
acceptance: Two stdio MCP plugin manifests exist under `plugins/context/` declaring `context.*` capability namespace and pinning npm-package versions for upstream Context7 + Repomix.
Two devtools install scripts exist under `scripts/devtools/` and warm npm cache for the pinned package versions.

One integration-test file `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs` declares `mod g5_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with two `#[tokio::test] async fn` tests; substring selectors `g5_plugin_manifests::context7_manifest_health_check` and `g5_plugin_manifests::repomix_manifest_health_check` resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests <selector>`.
Both verify scripts (P3-W10-F02 + P3-W10-F03) replace the TODO stubs with real implementations exiting 0 on a green run.

Both M1 (transport.command poison) and M2 (expected-tool-name regression) mutation contracts produce SA-tagged panics with the WO-0072 §5/§6 shape; restore-and-md5-confirm protocol verifies independence.
No `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes. No `tests/fixtures/**` modifications. No new feature-list.json entries. No new ADRs.
All commits Conventional-Commit-formatted with `Phase: 3` + correct `Feature:` + `Work-order: WO-0074` trailers.
ucil-daemon line coverage preserved at >= pre-WO baseline (>= 85% floor per master plan §15.4) measured via the `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov` workaround.
acceptance_criteria: python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="context7"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="context"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("context.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'

python3 -c 'import tomllib; m=tomllib.load(open("plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml","rb")); assert m["plugin"]["name"]=="repomix"; assert m["plugin"]["category"]=="context"; assert m["transport"]["type"]=="stdio"; assert any(c.startswith("context.") for c in m["capabilities"]["provides"])'
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests g5_plugin_manifests::context7_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'
cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g5_plugin_manifests g5_plugin_manifests::repomix_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'

! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/context/context7/plugin.toml plugins/context/repomix/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-context7-mcp.sh scripts/devtools/install-repomix-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F03.sh
! grep -qE 'TODO: implement acceptance test for P3-W10-F0[23]' scripts/verify/P3-W10-F02.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F03.sh
git diff main feat/WO-0074-context7-and-repomix-plugin-manifests -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/ | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
git diff main feat/WO-0074-context7-and-repomix-plugin-manifests -- tests/fixtures/ | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
git diff main feat/WO-0074-context7-and-repomix-plugin-manifests -- ucil-build/feature-list.json ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq
env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent' | awk '{ exit !($1+0 >= 85.0) }'
